label: the integer zero
gloss: the ~-class of (0,0): {p : Pair(N,N) | fst(p) = snd(p)}. That integer(integer-zero) holds is a theorem of the basis, and v0 has no proof layer to record it (design L1).
notes: Integers-as-equivalence-classes sketch (status: sketch): the classic quotient construction without quotient sorts - classes are Set(Pair(N,N)) values, class-hood is a predicate. Integer addition needs image-set formers v0 lacks (design L3).
